title: primula auriculata subsp. pycnorhiza
intro: Primula auriculata subsp. pycnorhiza, also known as the 'Mountain Auricula', is a plant species within the Primula genus, characterized by its unique, ear-shaped leaves and vibrant, yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal medicine, Primula auriculata subsp. pycnorhiza is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
